author: claude
content:
Adversarial robustness suite landed on branch `test/adversarial-robustness`.

## Scenarios generated

Browser-FREE (driven in-process against the real StorageController subprocess + DataSocket, or the asyncio server via raw sockets):
- S3 socket hostility: truncated body, garbage serialization tag, oversized (2GiB) length prefix, short header, wrong-arity record, non-sequence body, mid-message disconnect.
- S4 provider faults: transient store_record failure (recovers), permanent write_table failure.
- S6 malformed/hostile records: 2MiB strings, injection-y values, nested dicts, missing visit_id.
- S1/S5 storage-side: a store_record task that raises (same failure shape a crashing custom command / browser crash produces once records hit the controller).

Browser-REQUIRED (real TaskManager -> BrowserManager -> Firefox; skipped on env-block where no launchable Firefox, run for real in CI; NOT faked):
- S1a custom command execute() raises.
- S1b custom command hangs forever (timeout + kill).
- S5 browser process killed mid-visit (watchdog/BrowserManager restart).
- S2 crashing extension: documented as a design item (needs a deliberately-broken xpi); recovery shape already covered by S5.

## What the pipeline SURVIVES (passing tests)
- S3 socket hostility: server stays up after every hostile frame; a subsequent good visit still completes; no hang. The oversized-length-prefix frame does NOT hang shutdown.
- S4 transient: a later visit completes after a transient store failure (no global poisoning).
- S6: controller survives malformed records; good + hostile-but-valid visits complete.

## DEFECTS exposed (captured as xfail(strict=True) — the failing assertion IS the finding)

G1 — A raising `store_record` task strands the visit (DATA LOSS + potential HANG).
`StorageController.store_record` fires un-awaited asyncio tasks; the exception only surfaces when `finalize_visit_id` awaits them, AFTER popping them, BEFORE recording the completion token. Result: the visit is never enqueued to the completion_queue — neither on finalize NOR on shutdown (unfinalized/browser-crash case). A callback-bearing CommandSequence would hang forever and the visit is silently lost.
Tests: `test_raising_store_record_visit_still_finalizes`, `test_raising_store_record_unfinalized_visit_enqueued_on_shutdown`.

G2 — A permanent `write_table` fault loses completed visits.
The exception raises out of `flush_cache` during shutdown, killing the controller before the completion_queue is drained; structured-storage shutdown is skipped. Test: `test_permanent_write_table_fault_visit_still_completes`.

G3 — SQLite silently drops unknown-table/column records (pre-existing; this is #28/#30).
Confirmed directly: unknown table -> dropped, no table created; known table + one unknown column -> WHOLE record dropped (no partial save). Only logged as "Unsupported record". Reproduced by the probe; not duplicated as a new xfail since #28/#30 own it.

## Prioritized robustness gaps
1. [high] G1 store-task exception stranding — most severe: silent data loss + hang on the live crawl path. Fix is a design call (where/how to record the terminal state when the provider itself fails: count-and-continue vs fail-loud). needs-human.
2. [high] G3 SQLite silent drop — already #28/#30; same "fail-loud vs tolerate" decision; natural to resolve together with the SQLAlchemy rewrite (GH #1143).
3. [medium] G2 write_table fault — surface/count write failures instead of crashing the controller mid-drain.

Relates to: #28 / #30 (G3 silent drop), #45 (socket-reliability thread — S3 here is a first cut at wire-protocol fuzzing; #45 P3 echo-mode fuzzing would extend it).

Deliverable is the adversarial tests + findings; the G1/G2 fixes are design calls flagged needs-human. Full catalogue: `docs/developers/Adversarial-Robustness.md`.
